title: strychnos millepunctata
intro: Strychnos millepunctata, also known as the many-spotted nux vomica, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Loganiaceae. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and snake bites, due to its toxic alkaloids such as strychnine and brucine. The health benefits of Strychnos millepunctata include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of certain diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although its use requires caution due to its toxicity.
